name: Salsify API
what_it_does: Salsify REST API — product experience management platform for brands and retailers to manage, enrich, and distribute product content to digital channels and retail partners, enabling agents to automate product data workflows and retailer content syndication.
use_cases: Agents enriching product content in Salsify with AI-generated descriptions, alt text, and attribute values at scale
Retailer syndication — agents triggering Salsify content exports to retail partner channels (Amazon, Walmart, Target)
Digital shelf monitoring — agents querying Salsify for product content scores and identifying gaps in product data completeness
Asset management — agents uploading and tagging product images and videos in Salsify digital asset management
Channel readiness — agents checking content completeness against retailer-specific requirements before syndication
not_for: Small catalogs — Salsify is enterprise-priced; use Akeneo Community or Contentful for catalogs under 10K SKUs
Internal-only product data — Salsify's strength is retailer syndication; use simpler PIM for internal-only use cases
Real-time inventory or pricing — Salsify manages content, not transactional data; use ERP/OMS for inventory
best_when: You're a consumer brand managing product content across multiple retail partners and need to automate content enrichment and syndication workflows.
avoid_when: You don't sell through retail partners — Salsify's value is in retailer syndication network; use simpler PIM otherwise.

auth.notes: API key from Salsify account settings. Passed as Authorization header. Account and org-scoped access. API key tied to user permissions within the org. No OAuth for third-party integrations.

pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise: custom pricing based on SKU count and channels; typically $30K-$100K+/year
pricing.notes: Enterprise-only pricing via sales process. Significant investment for brands with large retail partner networks. ROI justified by scale of retailer syndication automation.

performance.rate_limits: 10 req/s per API key; bulk operations available; import/export jobs are async

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: API documentation is functional but less comprehensive than modern platforms. Error responses include codes and messages. Bulk operations (imports/exports) are async with job tracking. Property IDs required for data operations.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Product upsert by Salsify ID or external ID is idempotent. Property value updates are idempotent — last value wins. Product creation with same external ID returns existing product.
known_agent_gotchas: Salsify property IDs are system-generated GUIDs — agents must pre-fetch property definitions before writing product data
Bulk import/export jobs are async — agents must poll job status endpoint for completion before processing results
Organization ID required in all API paths — agents must include org ID extracted from account setup
Retailer channel configuration managed separately — API manages content but channel syndication configured in UI
Rate limit (10 req/s) requires pacing for large catalog operations — implement backoff for enterprise-scale imports

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II. GDPR compliant. API key with user-level permission scope. US data processing. No scope granularity on API keys. Enterprise security reviews available for large accounts.
